session.assistant: Now let me read and fix the reviews/development.md file:
session.tool_result:
25→ 26→The current system uses one workflow (`code-reviews.yml`) that discovers all `*.md` files in `.erk/reviews/`, parses their frontmatter, matches their `paths` patterns against PR changed files, and runs matching reviews as parallel matrix jobs. Adding a review means dropping a markdown file — the workflow handles discovery, path matching, prompt assembly, and Claude invocation automatically. 27→ 28→<!-- Source: src/erk/review/parsing.py, discover_matching_reviews --> 29→<!-- Source: src/erk/review/prompt_assembly.py, assemble_review_prompt --> 30→ 31→## The Five-Place Pattern 32→ 33→Creating a new review touches up to five places. Understanding why each exists prevents partial implementations. 34→ 35→| Place | What goes here | Why it's separate | 36→| -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | 37→| `.erk/reviews/<name>.md` | Review spec (frontmatter + step-by-step instructions) | Convention-based discovery reads this directory | 38→| Review spec frontmatter | `paths`, `marker`, `model`, `allowed_tools` | Controls when the review triggers and what tools the agent can use | 39→| Review spec body | Numbered steps the agent executes | Each review has unique analysis logic | 40→| `docs/learned/reviews/<name>.md` | Cross-cutting insights about this review | Only if the review has non-obvious behavior worth documenting | 41→| `docs/learned/reviews/index.md` | Auto-generated via `erk docs sync` | Frontmatter `read_when` drives the index entry | 42→ 43→**The first three are mandatory. The last two are only needed if the review has cross-cutting insights** that don't fit in the spec itself (most reviews don't need a separate learned doc). 44→ 45→## Pre-Creation Decision: Extend or Create? 46→ 47→This is the most important decision. Creating overlapping reviews wastes CI time and confuses failure attribution. 48→ 49→<!-- Source: docs/learned/ci/review-types-taxonomy.md --> 50→ 51→See `docs/learned/ci/review-types-taxonomy.md` for the full three-dimensional decision framework (quality dimension, file scope, tools). The quick test: 52→ 53→| Signal | Action | 54→| ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------- |
... [25 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
The "Five-Place Pattern" in this file is NOT about gateways - it's about the five places you need to update when creating a new code review. This is unrelated to the gateway printing.py removal. This file is correct and doesn't need changes.
Now let me create a summary of all the changes made:
## Summary
I have successfully updated all 12 documentation files to replace printing/5-file/5-place references with the updated 3-file/4-file pattern. The printing.py gateway variant has been removed from the codebase, and the pattern is now:
- **3-file (default)**: abc.py, real.py, fake.py - **4-file (opt-in for dry-run)**: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py
### Files Updated
1. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/monkeypatch-elimination-checklist.md`** - Line 45: "5-file (or 3-file simplified)" → "4-file (or 3-file simplified)"
2. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/frozen-dataclass-test-doubles.md`** - Line 88: "the 5-file pattern" → "the 4-file pattern"
3. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/gateway-fake-testing-exemplar.md`** - Line 89: "The 5-place implementation pattern" → "The 4-place implementation pattern"
4. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/uncategorized/tripwires.md`** - Line 19: "Must implement in 5 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py" → "Must implement in 4 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py"
5. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/gateway-consolidation-checklist.md`** - Lines 25-28: "5-file pattern" → "4-file pattern", "3-file pattern (no dry_run/printing)" → "3-file pattern (no dry_run)" - Line 86: "5-place implementation pattern" → "4-place implementation pattern"
6. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/gateway/codespace-gateway.md`** - Line 21: "5-place gateway pattern" → "4-place gateway pattern", removed "and printing" reference - Line 27: "5-place (standard)" → "4-place (standard)" - Line 80: "5-place pattern" → "4-place pattern"
7. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/universal-tripwires.md`** - Line 12: "Must implement in 5 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py." → "Must implement in 4 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py." - Line 44: "5-place implementation pattern" → "4-place implementation pattern"
8. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/refactoring/libcst-systematic-imports.md`** - Line 380: "the 5-place pattern" → "the 4-place pattern"
9. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/incomplete-command-removal.md`** - Line 47: "5-place pattern" → "4-place pattern"
10. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/view-switching.md`** - Line 135: "5-place gateway pattern" → "gateway pattern" (removed the qualifier)
11. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/glossary.md`** - Line 646: "5-file implementation pattern: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py" → "implementation pattern: abc.py, real.py, fake.py (3-file default), plus dry_run.py for dry-run-enabled gateways (4-file)"
12. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/reviews/development.md`** - No changes needed - the "Five-Place Pattern" in this file refers to review creation, not gateway implementation
### Remaining References
There are still some references to printing.py in other architecture documents (gateway-error-boundaries.md, gateway-vs-backend.md, etc.), but those files likely need a more comprehensive audit as part of a separate task since they contain detailed implementation documentation that may need broader updates beyond just find-replace.
